page: Microsoft Azure D64d_v4 is a general-purpose server belonging to the DDv4 family. It is powered by an Intel Xeon 8272CL x86_64 processor running at 2.6 GHz, providing 64 dedicated vCPUs across 32 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. The system is equipped with 256.0 GB of system memory, translating to 4.0 GB per core, and includes 2458 GB of bundled local storage. Virtualization is managed via the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor.

Performance benchmarks indicate strong multi-core capabilities, with stress-ng multi-core CPU performance and Passmark CPU extended instructions testing in the top 25%. Memory performance is also robust, showing strong results in large-block read-only and write bandwidth tests, alongside a strong Passmark memory score. However, single-core performance, static web serving throughput, and Redis SET operations fall within the average range. The server exhibits notable limitations in LLM text generation, scoring in the weak to poor categories for both 135M and 7B models.

Given its balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio and substantial bundled local storage, the D64d_v4 offers solid qualitative resource density for workloads requiring high multi-threaded compute and local data caching without the premium of memory-optimized tiers. It is well-suited for database operations, multi-threaded software compilation, and general-purpose enterprise applications. It is less optimal for GPU-dependent tasks or workloads requiring high-speed LLM text generation.
description: The Microsoft Azure D64d_v4 is a general-purpose server from the DDv4 family, powered by an Intel Xeon 8272CL x86_64 processor. It features 64 dedicated vCPUs, 256.0 GB of RAM, and 2458 GB of bundled local storage, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. Benchmarks show strong multi-core CPU performance and high memory bandwidth for large block operations, though single-core metrics and LLM text generation speeds remain average to weak. With a balanced 1:4 memory-to-vCPU ratio and substantial local disk capacity, this server provides a cost-efficient profile for multi-threaded workloads. It is highly suitable for database operations, enterprise applications, and software compilation tasks that benefit from local storage caching.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D64d_v4 is a general-purpose server featuring 64 vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and 2458 GB of local storage, delivering strong multi-core performance for database and enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D64d_v4 is a general-purpose server featuring 64 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and 2458 GB of bundled local storage.
tagline: A balanced multi-core server featuring dedicated Intel processors and substantial local storage for demanding database and enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 64 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 8272CL processor
256 GB of system memory and 2458 GB of bundled local storage
Strong multi-core CPU and large-block memory bandwidth performance
Optimized for database operations and multi-threaded enterprise workloads
Virtualization powered by Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor
